title: This Concord mom is making her dream come true by running Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kelly Turner, 43, from Concord, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
Turner aims to fulfill a long-held dream while supporting a charity that serves underserved communities.
She overcame challenges including single parenthood and Lyme disease to train for the marathon.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her best friend
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Brette Miller is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late best friend, Angelle Lozano.
Lozano, aged 29, died from a ruptured brain aneurysm in the previous year.
Miller is fundraising for The Brain Aneurysm Foundation, supporting research and early detection efforts.

title: Clover Food Lab puts itself on the market
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is seeking a buyer after filing a notice to potentially lay off 182 workers if a sale fails.
The company, with 11 restaurants and catering service, emerged from bankruptcy in 2024 with reduced operations.
CEO Julia Wrin Poper aims to comply with regulations and maintain Clover's locally sourced fast food offerings.

title: ‘BPS transportation is failing us’: School bus delays have Boston parents calling for change
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Public Schools buses fall short of on-time standards, with 93% morning and 88% afternoon on-time rates in March.
BPS transportation's $200 million annual budget is under scrutiny due to persistent delays and complex routing issues faced by families.
Parents report significant daily disruptions, including missed work and alternative transport arrangements, due to frequent bus delays.

title: How did the ‘Seger bar’ guy get Gov. Healey for his latest video?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey appeared in a video with Watertown native Brian Daly, known for his Bob Seger bar reviews.
The video featuring Healey was filmed at 21st Amendment bar in Beacon Hill, where they enjoyed drinks and Seger's music.
Daly and Healey's collaboration happened by chance after they met previously at a Local 103 union anniversary event.

title: Michelin-recommended dumpling chain Nan Xiang to open in Framingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: New York-based Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings to launch its first Massachusetts location in Framingham on Route 9.
The restaurant, known for handmade dumplings, will open in the former Fresh Market space near Shoppers World.
Nan Xiang Express, the chain's fast-casual arm, already operates in Boston's Chinatown and Shrewsbury.

title: ‘The Drama’ review: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson’s darkly funny Boston movie is designed to discomfort
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Drama', starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, is a dark comedy filmed in Boston exploring social norms and taboos.
Director Kristoffer Borgli examines cancel culture and societal forgiveness through the film's provocative characters and storyline.
The movie, set to release April 3, highlights tense pre-wedding dynamics and the challenge of confronting past secrets.

title: National UMass survey has bad news for Trump
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: UMass Amherst poll shows Trump's approval rating at 33%, the lowest in his second term.
Poll indicates Trump's support eroded among men, working-class Americans, and Black voters.
Economic concerns and disapproval of foreign policy actions contribute to Trump's declining approval.

title: Globe Magazine names New England’s 50 fastest-growing companies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The Boston Globe and Statista released a list of New England's 50 fastest-growing companies in 2026.
Top-performing industries include medical equipment, AI weather forecasting, IT, fintech, hospitality, and customizable products.
Eligibility required $100,000 revenue in 2021, $1.5 million in 2024, and independent New England headquarters.

title: Judge denies Lindsay Clancy’s request for two-part murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge William Sullivan denied Lindsay Clancy's request for a two-part trial in her murder case.
Clancy's trial will address both her alleged guilt and mental state in the July proceedings.
Clancy is charged with the 2023 murders of her three children, citing postpartum mental illness as a defense.
